headlines.title: POLICE FIRE UPON STRIKING MILLMEN
headlines.subtitle: Greek Shot During Officers' Rush to Dispel Laborers From Hilltop.
headlines.fullText:
Associated Press,
CLINTON, Mass, Jun- 3-One man was wounded by a bullet from a po- liceman's revolves during a fight he- Tween officers and a band of striking Lancaster maill Workers today. It is alleged that a Greek striker brought a woman to work in the mill. the woman was rough- ly treated by a crowd of strikers but her assistance, brought a policeman to the scene. a large band of police was rushed to the yard of the Church of Our Lady of the Rosary The strikers took up a position on the officers during which many were hurt. The officers charged up the hill and fired a volley into the mir, but this demonstratie man did not appear to alarm the strikers. Then the officers leveled their weapons at the strikers and charged up the hill a third time, a few shots being fired on the way. One shot woundeda OrtaWeaver in the les. The dis- turbern were dispersed.

headlines.title: FIRST SURVEY MADE ON COTTON BELT TERMINAL
headlines.subtitle: Chief Engineer Purdon and As- sistants Arrive From St. Louis and Tyler.
headlines.fullText:
Preliminary work was begun Mon- day towards the construction of the new Cotton Belt freight terminals on Ninth street, when ( D. Turion, chief engineer St. Louis, and W. T. Eaton. chief engineer at Tyler, arrived in Fort Worth,
The recent sale of $7,500,000 first ter- malnal and unifying mortgage five pier expenditure of $2.250.000 for termine in Fort Worth and St. Louis, and the visit of the engineers here at this time is in connection with building at the proposed terminals and freight depot.
The engineers were accompanied ww a corpa of assistants, who will do the actual construction survey of the necessary work for the depot and added trackage in yards to handle the care to and from the freight depot.
The work to be done date by the engineers will call for the expenditure of nearly $1.500,000 in Fort Worth.

headlines.title: Children Asked to Repeat Tom Thumb Wedding Ceremony
headlines.fullText:
A second Tom Thumb wedding." similar to that which took place last werk at the Boulevard Methodist north side, will be held at the Mekin- ley Avenue Methodist Church Tuesday. June 4. One of the stars, will be JAMES M'GREGOR.
There will be a wedding march ser- eral -best men" and the usual dainty arrary of bridesmaids-only the bridesmaids in this case will wear short dresden
The ceremony held last Tuesday was successful from every point of view.

headlines.title: 500 EAT JEFFERSON DAVIS. DAY DINNER
headlines.subtitle: Daughters of Confederacy Give Picnic for Veterans at Forest Park.
headlines.fullText:

headlines.title: A. & M. COLLEGE TO BE REBUILT AT ONCE
headlines.subtitle: Governor Colpquitt to Set Aside De- ficiency Appropriation to Pra- vide Funds.
headlines.fullText:
AUSTIN, June 3. Governor Col- quitt tutte announced during the meeting of the A. & M. board of di- rectors today, that he would set aside a deficiency dentiene appropriation to tecon- hall and that it would get gary to wait on the next legislature for an appropriation.
The banks of the state will be asked to cash the deficiency warrants that work may proceed without de- tay. Plans for the building are to be outlined and submitted at a meeting of the directorate next Monday.
.. The burned building cost about $100.000. The few structure probably will be larger and more costly.

headlines.title: GERMAN SQUADRON WELCOMED BY TAFT
headlines.subtitle: Visiting Warships at Hampton Roads Return American Fleet's Visit.
headlines.fullText:
fanartated Press.
FORT MONROE, Va., June 3 -Pres- rigtat visiting functions German that warships will end here only today when the squadron weighs anchor for home.
The German emperor . three be fighting machines that have come to HeUnited States to return the Amer- Hen dieet's visit to Kiel last year, came up to Hampton Roads from Lynn Have Hay early this morning, es- corte! by ships of Admiral Winslow's third division of the Atlantic fleet. Both squadrons roared ou! a twenty- one-gun salute as the Mayflower pleamed into the roads with President Taft and the German ambassador, Count von Bernadorff, on board.
Carly this morning the American ships quietly slipped their cables and Beaded down for future ,The . it 92 gliding by the Virginis Capes to meet the visitors.

headlines.title: STRIKERS CAUSE RIOT
headlines.fullText:
NEWARK, X."J. June 2-A pitched battle beas con carne departments Be Heine Laborers and man from Louisiana and a strong He main Street of the town today. One Hoe was fatally shot
During a preliminary skirmish the laborers felled Chief of Police Tolen cobblestone and pursued him in a vacant house, from which he opened fire upon them. The rioters bombarded the house with' stones and bricks for several minutes before the reserves arrived,

Materials are high-grade percale and good quality linene in nearly all desirable plain shades. They are extra well made and very prettily trimmed with pearl buttons and striped bias folds and pipings in contrasting shades.

Many styles are included in this offering and all are smart and snappy. Materials are princess welt in plain white, pink, blue and tan and fine quality percale in white and black or white and colored stripes. Nearly all have large sailor collars and French cuffs. Those made of princess welt are trimmed with braid and crochet buttons; those of per- cale are trimmed with collar, cuffs, pipings and folds of plain material in contrasting shades.

Misses' junior girls and small women will all surely want to possess one or more of these. They are strictly high-class tai- Tored garments, especially suit- able for street wear. The as- sortment includes nearly two dozen different styles, all at- tractively trimmed in the sea- son's most novel and pleasing effects. Materials are mostly fine gingham in stripes and broken plaids, striped voiles and plain white or colored pique and princess welt.
